rulings.Case Title: JOSEPHINE WYMAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 14, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT NIBCO INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: NANCY MACHADO et al VS. AMERICAN ASBESTOS CO., et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 14, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT UNION OIL COMPANY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF BECAUSE IT DID NOT ADDRESS THE ALLEGED WORK DECEDANT DID AT DEFENDANT'S FACILITIES PRIOR TO 1980. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO MOSTES' DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED; PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO KAMM DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED; PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO MCMAHON DECLARATIONS ARE SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. OBJECTIONS ONE THROUGH FOUR ARE OVERRULED GIVEN THE CERTIFICATION; OBJECTION EIGHT IS SUSTAINED, OBJECTION NINE IS OVERRULED. OBJECTIONS FIVE AND SIX RELATE TO PUBLIC DOCUMENTS AND ARE OVERRULED. THE COURT DOES NOT UNDERSTAND THE BASIS FOR ADMITTING THE COHEN DEPOSITION TRANSCRIPT AND FOR THAT REASON, OBJECTION SEVEN IS SUSTAINED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL A. VANDER WAERDT VS. CHARLES M. COLLINS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 14, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANT CHARLES COLLINS'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: HEARING REQUIRED ON DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN PETERSON et al VS. AC AND S, INC et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 14, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANT YARWAY CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT FAILS TO IDENTIFY DEPOSITION QUESTIONS REGARDING DEFENDANT OR ITS PRODUCTS; DEFENDANT FAILS TO COMPLY WITH C.R.C. 3.1350(D); AND DEFENDANT FAILED TO OBTAIN RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN PETERSON et al VS. AC AND S, INC et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 14, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANT I.T.T. CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. THE DECEDENT'S TESTIMONY, WITH ITS MINIMAL REFERENCE TO DEFENDANT, IS NOT ADMISSIBLE AGAINST DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S STANDARD INTERROGATORIES FAIL TO MENTION DEFENDANT. THE HEIRS OF DECEDENT HAVE NO KNOWLEDGE OF DEFENDANT. ALTHOUGH SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES ARE DESIRABLE, THEY ARE NOT MANDATORY AND APART FROM SPECULATION, THERE IS NO INDICATION HOW PLAINTIFF COULD PROVE ITS CASE AGAINST DEFENDANT. =(302/PJM/VC) COURT AMENDED AND ISSUED A LATE TENTATIVE RULING ON MAY 14, 2008. IT IS AS FOLLOWS: Amended Tentative Ruling Petersen v. AC& S Inc. #454146 ITT¿s motion is denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. By serving the special interrogatories, defendant recognized the need to do so. Plaintiffs¿ objection was timely filed and apparently bona fide; yet, defendant took no steps to correct its defective service. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLEY BROWN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 14, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT BUFFALO PUMPS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE FIRST SENTENCE OF PARAGRAPH SIX OF THE BROWN DECLARATION IS STRUCK AS HEARSAY. THE REMAINING OBJECTIONS TO THE BROWN DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED AS ARE THE OBJECTIONS TO BROWN'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY. THE RECORD FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT THAT PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS THAT WAS MANUFACTURED OR SOLD BY DEFENDANT. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY HAS REJECTED THE THEORY THAT DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO WARN ABOUT THE USE OF OTHERS' ASBESTOS REPLACEMENT PARTS IS A BASIS FOR LIABILITY. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: WARREN HARRINGTON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 14, 2008 LINE 14. DEFENDANT STA-RITE INDUSTRIES, L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. THE WHITSON DECLARATION LACKS FOUNDATION AND PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JOEL ASSEKO et al VS. GUARDSMARK GP LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 14, 2008 LINE 13. DEFENDANT GUARDSMARK, L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. HEARING REQUIRED TO DETERMINE WHETHER TO GRANT LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE FACTS PLED REFLECT ONLY AN AMENDMENT TO THE DECEMBER 1, 2001 CONTRACT FOR THE TERM TO NOVEMBER 30, 2004. AS SUCH, IT IS NOT A CONTRACT ENTERED INTO, RENEWED OR EXTENDED WITHIN THE MEANING OF LABOR CODE SECTION 19134. THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT INTENDED TO INDUCE PLAINTIFFS TO TAKE ACTION OR RELIANCE BY PLAINTIFF. THE TENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO PLEAD A FIDUCIARY OR CONFIDENTIAL RELATIONSHIP. AN EMPLOYER - EMPLOYEE RELATIONSHIP IS INSUFFICIENT. ODORIZZI V BLOOMFIELD SCHOOL DISTRICT (1966) 246 CAL APP 2D 23, 129. =(302/PJM/AL)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: GOODRICH & PENNINGTON MORTGAGE FUND, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn For Attorneys' Fees On Appeal
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 14, 2008 LINE 11. RESPONDENT ADVANTA MORTGAGE CORP. U.S.A. AND ADVANTA MORTGAGE CONDUIT SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION For Attorneys' Fees On Appeal is granted awarding attorneys fees of $348,736 and costs of $13,209.11. The Court has reduced the request as follows: Review of notice of appeal: reduce Biderman time by 2 hours at $320 and Klinges time by 3 hours at $500 = $2140 Motion to post a bond: reduce Biderman time by 15 hours and Klinges time by 6 hours; $7800 No reduction is made for the writ or the brief on appeal given the significance of the issues and the complexitiy of the case. Preparation for oral argument: the argument was made by Klinges; the time of Biderman is reduced by 20 hours at $340 and by Krabil $20 hours at $450 = $15,800 The request of $374,476 is reduced by $25,740 and the amount awarded is $348,736. The Court finds that the hourly rates are consistent with market rates for complex litigation of the kind presented by this case and the work of the Howard Rice firm, acting as local counsel, was necessary and reasonable. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Of Respondent'S Demurrer And Demurrer
